io.h(3) Library Functions Manual io.h(3)

NAME

io.h - Capture stdout/stderr and redirect to logging system.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct log_io_t
Opaque handle for IO capture state.

Macros


#define LOG_IO(prefix, block)
Capture output from a code block and log it.

Functions


log_io_t log_io_start (void)
Start capturing stdout and stderr. void log_io_stop (log_io_t capture, const char *prefix)
Stop capturing and log the accumulated output.

Detailed Description

Capture stdout/stderr and redirect to logging system.

Provides functions and macros to capture stdout and stderr output and redirect it to the logging system instead of suppressing it.

Usage: // Capture specific code: log_io_t capture = log_io_start(); ffmpeg_function_that_prints_stuff(); log_io_stop(capture, "ffmpeg");

// Capture a code block (recommended): LOG_IO("ffmpeg", { ffmpeg_function_that_prints_stuff(); });

Definition in file io.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define LOG_IO( prefix, block)

Value:

do {                                                                        \


  log_io_t _log_io = log_io_start();                                       \


  block;                                                                    \


  log_io_stop(_log_io, (prefix));                                         \
} while (0)




Capture output from a code block and log it. This macro redirects
    stdout and stderr for the duration of the code block, then logs any captured
    output with the given prefix.




Example: LOG_IO("ffmpeg", { av_log_set_callback(NULL);
    // Use default ffmpeg logging process_file("input.mp4"); });




Parameters


prefix Prefix for log messages (const char *)


block Code block to capture (must be valid C statement block)


Note


Prefix and block are only evaluated once



Variables in the enclosing scope are accessible in the block




Definition at line 81 of file io.h.


81 #define LOG_IO(prefix, block)                                                 \
82   do {                                                                        \
83     log_io_t _log_io = log_io_start();                                       \
84     block;                                                                    \
85     log_io_stop(_log_io, (prefix));                                         \
86   } while (0)






Referenced by audio_start_duplex(),
    audio_stop_duplex(), audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    ffmpeg_decoder_create(), ffmpeg_decoder_create_stdin(),
    ffmpeg_encoder_create(), ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(),
    gpg_sign_with_key(), h265_decoder_create(),
    h265_encoder_destroy(), and yt_dlp_is_available().








Function
  Documentation




log_io_t log_io_start (void )


Start capturing stdout and stderr. Redirects both stdout and
    stderr to a pipe so their output can be captured and logged. Call
    log_io_stop() to end capture and log the accumulated output.




Returns


Capture handle (use with log_io_stop)


Note


If capture fails, returns a handle with saved_stdout_fd =
  -1



Do not use stdout/stderr directly while capturing




Definition at line 15 of file io.c.


15                             {
16   log_io_t capture = {.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
17 
18   // Save original stdout and stderr
19   capture.saved_stdout_fd = dup(STDOUT_FILENO);
20   capture.saved_stderr_fd = dup(STDERR_FILENO);
21 
22   if (capture.saved_stdout_fd < 0 || capture.saved_stderr_fd < 0) {
23     if (capture.saved_stdout_fd >= 0)
24       close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
25     if (capture.saved_stderr_fd >= 0)
26       close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
27     return (log_io_t){.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
28   }
29 
30   // Create a pipe for capturing output
31   int pipefd[2];
32 #ifdef _WIN32
33   if (_pipe(pipefd, LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE, _O_BINARY) < 0) {
34 #else
35   if (pipe(pipefd) < 0) {
36 #endif
37     close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
38     close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
39     return (log_io_t){.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
40   }
41 
42   // pipefd[0] is read end, pipefd[1] is write end
43   int write_fd = pipefd[1];
44   capture.pipe_fd = pipefd[0];
45 
46 #ifndef _WIN32
47   // Make the read end non-blocking so we can drain without hanging
48   int flags = fcntl(capture.pipe_fd, F_GETFL, 0);
49   if (flags >= 0) {
50     fcntl(capture.pipe_fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK);
51   }
52 
53   // Make the write end non-blocking. LOG_IO redirects the process-global stderr,
54   // so ALL threads write to this pipe. If the pipe buffer fills (64KB on Linux),
55   // blocking writes would stall other threads and potentially deadlock (e.g. x265
56   // internal threads block on stderr while avcodec_open2 can't return). Non-blocking
57   // writes silently drop excess output (EAGAIN) which is acceptable for diagnostics.
58   int wflags = fcntl(write_fd, F_GETFL, 0);
59   if (wflags >= 0) {
60     fcntl(write_fd, F_SETFL, wflags | O_NONBLOCK);
61   }
62 #endif
63 
64   // Redirect both stdout and stderr to the pipe
65   if (dup2(write_fd, STDOUT_FILENO) < 0 || dup2(write_fd, STDERR_FILENO) < 0) {
66     close(write_fd);
67     close(capture.pipe_fd);
68     close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
69     close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
70     return (log_io_t){.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
71   }
72 
73   // Close the write end in the parent process (we only need it in redirected streams)
74   close(write_fd);
75 
76   return capture;
77 }






References LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE, log_io_t::pipe_fd,
    log_io_t::saved_stderr_fd, and log_io_t::saved_stdout_fd.






void
  log_io_stop (log_io_t capture, const char * prefix)


Stop capturing and log the accumulated output. Restores stdout and
    stderr to their original state, reads all captured output from the pipe, and
    logs it at INFO level with the given prefix.




Parameters


capture Capture handle returned by
  log_io_start()


prefix Prefix to use in log messages (e.g., "ffmpeg",
  "portaudio")


Note


Safe to call with invalid handle (saved_stdout_fd = -1)



After calling this, the capture handle is invalidated




Definition at line 79 of file io.c.


79                                                        {
80   if (capture.saved_stdout_fd < 0 || capture.saved_stderr_fd < 0) {
81     return;  // Capture was not started successfully
82   }
83 
84   // Flush stdout/stderr BEFORE restoring to ensure all output is in the pipe
85   fflush(stdout);
86   fflush(stderr);
87 
88   // Restore stdout and stderr
89   dup2(capture.saved_stdout_fd, STDOUT_FILENO);
90   dup2(capture.saved_stderr_fd, STDERR_FILENO);
91   close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
92   close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
93 
94   // Read and log the captured output
95   if (capture.pipe_fd >= 0) {
96     char buffer[LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE];
97     ssize_t bytes_read;
98 
99     // Read all available data from the pipe
100     while ((bytes_read = read(capture.pipe_fd, buffer, sizeof(buffer) - 1)) > 0) {
101       buffer[bytes_read] = '\0';
102 
103       // Split by lines and log each line
104       char *line_start = buffer;
105       char *line_end;
106 
107       while ((line_end = strchr(line_start, '\n')) != NULL) {
108         // Null-terminate the line (overwriting the newline)
109         *line_end = '\0';
110 
111         // Log the line if it's not empty
112         if (line_start[0] != '\0') {
113           if (prefix) {
114             log_debug("[%s] %s", prefix, line_start);
115           } else {
116             log_debug("%s", line_start);
117           }
118         }
119 
120         // Move to the next line
121         line_start = line_end + 1;
122       }
123 
124       // Log any remaining partial line
125       if (line_start[0] != '\0') {
126         if (prefix) {
127           log_debug("[%s] %s", prefix, line_start);
128         } else {
129           log_debug("%s", line_start);
130         }
131       }
132     }
133 
134     close(capture.pipe_fd);
135   }
136 }






References log_debug, LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE,
    log_io_t::pipe_fd, log_io_t::saved_stderr_fd, and
    log_io_t::saved_stdout_fd.
